content.text: Selling digital downloads in Pakistan is a nightmare, but it doesn't have to be. You've likely tried everything from PayPal to Stripe, only to be blocked due to geographic restrictions or banking limitations. The problem is real, and it's not just you. It's the same pain point for thousands of digital creators in restricted countries, including Ghana. If you're tired of being stuck and want to actually get paid for your digital products, it's time to take a closer look at the real solution.
The Real Reason This Problem Exists

The reason you can't sell digital downloads in Pakistan (or Ghana, or any other restricted country) is because of the way the conventional payment processors are set up. They're designed to cater to the needs of the majority, not the minority. As a result, they've created a system that's hostile to creators from certain regions. It's not a coincidence that you're being blocked; it's a deliberate choice made by the payment processors to prioritize their own interests over yours.
Why the Obvious Fixes Do Not Work

You might be thinking that using a VPN or a proxy server will solve the problem, but it won't. These temporary fixes only mask the issue and won't give you the long-term solution you need. You might also try looking for alternative payment processors, but they'll often have the same restrictions and limitations as the ones you're trying to avoid. The truth is, the conventional payment processors are designed to be restrictive, and there's no getting around it.
